channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: JPMorgan Built a Pipeline of Female CEO Candidates That Was the Envy of Wall Street. How Did It Fall Apart?
item.description: Marianne Lake's retirement from JPMorgan ended the bank's chance to place a woman in one of the world's top jobs.

item.title: Three Dads Started Selling Hats from a Garage with $750—Now They've Sold $35 Million, Partnered with Gary Vee, and Built a Fatherhood Community
item.description: What began as three friends discussing fatherhood struggles evolved into Dad Gang, a brand that has sold $35 million worth of products.

item.title: Even as Elon Musk Calls Philanthropy 'Very Hard,' Everyday Americans Gave a Record $617 Billion—Despite Cost of Living Squeeze
item.description: Individual donations reached $394 billion last year, with a 17% rise in bequests signaling a shift driven by the Great Wealth Transfer.

item.title: A third-generation Texas oilman turned an organic farming company into a leading advanced nuclear startup at a small Christian college
item.description: A look at the effort to scale up next-generation nuclear power to meet the demands of the AI data center boom.
